completedTraceFiberFormStatus.label: completed trace-fiber form domain
completedTraceFiberFormStatus.reason: The Green lift is considered on the completed Volterra trace-fiber form domain with feature and trace norms in the graph norm.

boundaryMinimalityInputStatus.label: Euler boundary/minimality input
boundaryMinimalityInputStatus.reason: The imported boundary theorem gives Q(f_x,h)=0 on ker R and identifies the endpoint concomitant with the same Euler residual.

hardyMultiplierIdentityStatus.label: symbolic Hardy multiplier identity
hardyMultiplierIdentityStatus.reason: The lifted minus branch is exactly multiplication of the lifted plus branch by kappa(s,u)=(1-s-u)/(1+s+u).

pointwiseMultiplierBoundStatus.label: pointwise multiplier bound
pointwiseMultiplierBoundStatus.reason: The symbolic multiplier theorem proves |kappa(s,u)|<=1 for s,u>=0.

compressedGreenLiftContractionStatus.label: compressed Green-lift contraction
compressedGreenLiftContractionStatus.reason: The branch transport has algebraic form C K E.  The endpoint concomitant vanishes by Euler minimality, and K has pointwise norm at most one, so the compressed Green-lift map is contractive on the completed trace image.

proof: Use the completed Volterra trace-fiber form domain for the Green lift.
Apply Euler-Lagrange minimality to get Q(f_x,h)=0 for h in ker R.
Use the Green boundary theorem to identify the endpoint concomitant with that residual.
Apply the symbolic identity F_-=K F_+ with |K|<=1.
Conclude ||C K E||<=1 on the completed Green-minimizer trace image.
